Explanation: Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the 'contact' table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and cause maintenance issues if the schema changes. Explicitly list the required columns, e.g., `context.select(CONTACT.NAME, CONTACT.PHONE, CONTACT.EMAIL).from(CONTACT).fetch()`.

Code fragment: public final TableField<KvstoreRecord, LocalDateTime> LASTMODIFIED = createField(DSL.name("lastModified"), SQLDataType.LOCALDATETIME(6).defaultValue(DSL.field("CURRENT_TIMESTAMP", SQLDataType.LOCALDATETIME)), this, "");
Explanation: The LASTMODIFIED column has a default value (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) but is marked as NULLABLE, which means it can never be NULL in practice since it will always default to the current timestamp. Consider adding .nullable(false) to the column definition to accurately reflect its non-null nature.

Using selectFrom(KVSTORE) fetches all columns from the KVSTORE table, but only the VALUE column is actually used. Replace with ctx.select(KVSTORE.VALUE).from(KVSTORE) to explicitly select only the needed column.

Using selectFrom("contact") fetches all columns from the contact table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Replace with an explicit column selection like ctx.select(CONTACT.ID, CONTACT.NAME, ...).from(CONTACT) to fetch only the required columns.
